mobileMenu.headline: Let’s give cultural exploration its voice.

handset.title: The Handset
handset.desc: Screen-free AI audio companion for museums.

hero.label: Next-gen audio guides
hero.subtitle: Tree'd is an AI-powered audio guide system that doesn't just play prerecorded stories. Visitors have two-way conversations. They ask questions and get answers instantly, in their language.

hero.desc: Tree'd turns any museum visit into a self-paced, immersive experience where our AI-powered audio guide responds in real time, in your language.

ecosystem.label: The Ecosystem
ecosystem.title1: A new standard in interactive museum guides,

handset.title: The Handset
handset.desc: A screen-free, AI-powered companion. Just lift and ask—it answers naturally in any language.

ai.title: Conversational AI
ai.desc: Visitors converse with a guide trained on your collection, not a script. Two-way, in any of 12+ languages.

multi.title: Multilingual Guide
multi.desc: Native-level dialogue in 12+ languages, removing all barriers.

instant.title: Instant Journey
instant.desc: No app download. No account. No screen. Visitors pick up the Handset and start exploring.

tap.title: Tap & Listen
tap.desc: Intuitive engagement. Simply tap the Handset to a sensor to connect.

cta.title: Start with a 90-day pilot.
cta.desc: Turn every visit into a conversation and experience the power of conversational AI in person. Let's build the future of museum storytelling together.

advantages.label: The Tree'd Advantage
advantages.title: Why Museums
advantages.desc: We don't just provide hardware; we turn your collection into something visitors can interact with.

edu.title: Educational Mission
edu.desc: Increases dwell time, number of exhibits visited, and total interaction and deepens visitor engagement across the entire tour.

finance.title: Financial Health
finance.desc: Adds a source of income through a revenue-sharing model — boosting earnings per visitor without added staff or tech overhead.

reach.title: Reach & Satisfaction
reach.desc: Attracts broader audiences through multilingual tours and boosts key metrics such as NPS and visitor satisfaction.

tech.title: Responsible Tech
tech.desc: Hardware housed in biodegradable plastic with high-efficiency, low-energy technology designed for responsible operations.

footer.tagline: Conversational audio guides for museums. Powered by AI to create more immersive experiences.
footer.rights: Tree'd History Guide by ZedZag B.V. — All rights reserved.

hero.desc: Tree'd gives visitors the freedom to explore at their own pace, guided by their own curiosity. Instead of following a fixed script, they simply ask questions and hear meaningful answers.

step1.title: Select your language
step1.desc: Start your journey by tapping your Handset on the NFC Language Panel to choose the language for the tour.

step2.title: Choose an exhibit
step2.desc: Exhibits have an NFC chip nearby. Just tap it with the Handset, and you'll instantly hear the story behind the piece.

step3.title: Start a conversation
step3.desc: Have a question? Just ask. The Handset listens, understands what you're asking, and responds with museum-approved knowledge.

benefits.title: Designed for real curiosity.
benefits.subtitle: Tree'd is an audio guide that adapts to visitors, not the other way around.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.heading: Tree'd vs Audio Guides, Tour Guides, and Apps
comparison.intro: Audio guides can't answer questions. Live tours are not self-paced. Apps pull attention away from the art. Tree'd fixes all three.

engagementDifference.desc: Traditional audio guides deliver fixed narration. Conversations change how visitors engage. When visitors can ask questions and receive answers in real time, they spend more time with each artwork, explore more pieces, and engage more deeply with the collection.

cta.label: Museum Partnership
cta.desc: Give your visitors a reason to dive deep and truly connect with your collection. Join the next generation of guided tours.

step1.title: TOUR START
step1.desc: Pick up your screen-free Handset. No apps, no downloads—just curiosity. You're ready to explore the museum at your own pace.

step2.title: NFC TAP
step2.desc: Found something interesting? Simply tap the Handset to the NFC tag near the exhibit that caught your attention. The story begins instantly in your ears.

step3.title: STORY PLAYING
step3.desc: Once activated, a short, museum-curated audio story begins playing through the Handset, giving the visitor essential context about the exhibit.

step4.title: ASK ANYTHING
step4.desc: Have a specific question? Just ask. Our AI understands your context and provides museum-approved answers in real-time.

step5.title: AI RESPONSE
step5.desc: The Handset responds naturally, deepening your connection to the art. Every visit becomes a unique, two-way conversation.

hero.label: The Handset
hero.desc: An AI-powered museum audio guide device that lets visitors listen to curated stories and ask follow-up questions in real time, in their language.

internal.title: Intelligence inside.
internal.desc: Powered by a custom conversational AI model, the Handset processes requests locally to ensure the fastest possible response time and complete visitor privacy.

dialogue.title: Dynamic Dialogue
dialogue.desc: Speak naturally to the guide. It understands context, follows your curiosity, and responds with human-like intelligence.

presence.title: Focused Presence
presence.desc: Designed to keep your eyes on the art, not a phone. Pure audio immersion without digital distraction.

interaction.title: Engineered for museums.
interaction.desc: The Handset introduces a familiar yet strikingly modern form, inspired by classic telephones. Its screenless design removes distractions, creating an undisrupted connection between visitors and the art.

tap.title: Simple Tap to Start
tap.desc: Just tap an exhibit to begin. No apps, no QR codes, no friction.

audio.title: Premium Audio Output
audio.desc: Silky smooth audio quality for a premium, focused visitor experience.

capabilities.title: Effortless intelligence, built for all-day visits.
capabilities.desc: The Handset houses a multilingual conversational AI that answers every question in real time, in the visitor's language.

battery.title: 6+ Hours
battery.desc: Active use on a single charge.

formFactors.label: Form Factors
formFactors.title: One system. Any form.
formFactors.subtitle: The experience remains exceptional, the form adapts to your museum.

handset.title: The Handset
handset.desc: Our flagship. Instantly familiar and intuitive to use.

box.title: The Box
box.desc: Compact and minimal. Built for AUX-first experiences.

watch.title: The Watch
watch.desc: AUX-first with easier NFC scanning on the move.

custom.title: The Custom
custom.desc: Customize the shape and color to suit your space, your visitors, your story.

slides.title: Engineered for Precision
slides.desc: Built with tight tolerances and premium materials to ensure long-term durability and reliability for heavy museum usage.

slides.title: Real-Time Intelligence
slides.desc: Optimized processing power delivers fast, natural conversations that allow visitors to ask follow-up questions without any delay.

slides.title: Built for Global Audiences
slides.desc: Advanced multilingual support allows for natural dialogue across dozens of languages without the need for separate device versions.

slides.title: Magnetic Docking System
slides.desc: Snaps seamlessly into the Tree through magnetic plugs for effortless charging and intuitive placement for both staff and visitors.

slides.title: Instant Content Sync
slides.desc: Push firmware updates, new points of interests or language, and content refinements remotely and sync every Handset in seconds without physical intervention.

slides.title: Understand Visitor Behavior
slides.desc: Track detailed engagement metrics and exhibit performance through our comprehensive dashboard to gain deep insights into visitor interests.

slides.title: All-Day Battery Performance
slides.desc: High-capacity power engineered to support museum operating hours without interruption, ensuring every visitor gets a full experience.

cta.title: Bring Your Exhibits to Life
cta.desc: Ready to give your visitors an unforgettable, conversational journey? Let's discuss how the Handset fits your museum's story. 90-day pilots available.

explodedView.para1: Every component is placed with intent, maintaining the iconic silhouette while maximizing performance.
explodedView.para2: A complex ecosystem of hardware, housed in a form factor that feels natural and familiar.

hero.label: Sustainability
hero.desc: Tree'd is built from the ground up to minimize waste, maximize longevity, and respect our resources. We believe the future of technology shouldn't cost the earth.

plastic.title: Biodegradable Plastic
plastic.desc: Tree'd Handsets are 3D printed using biodegradable materials, reducing end-of-life waste. Modular components allow batteries and parts to be replaced, not discarded.

local.title: Infrastructure
local.desc: All AI processing runs on The Tree, our on-site hub. No cloud queries. No external data transfer. Local processing reduces the carbon footprint of every visitor interaction.

ai.title: Closed Universe AI
ai.desc: Efficient design ensures every response is relevant, reducing unnecessary computation and avoiding the energy waste of general-purpose models.

deepDive.label: Responsible Tech
deepDive.title: Efficient by Design.
deepDive.desc: Local processing reduces energy consumption compared to constant cloud communication. Modular hardware extends product lifespan. Every component is designed to minimize waste while maintaining performance at scale.

localProcessing.title: Local Processing
localProcessing.desc: Processing data locally significantly reduces the carbon footprint compared to constant cloud data transmission.

longevity.title: Longevity & Repair
longevity.desc: Modular components allow for easy repairs and battery replacements, keeping devices in use longer and out of landfills.

responsible.title: Responsible Technology for Cultural Spaces
responsible.desc: Tree'd is designed to operate within cultural spaces without adding digital or environmental noise. Screen-free interaction keeps attention on the collection. No disposable batteries reduce ongoing waste. Local processing eliminates constant cloud traffic. Modular hardware ensures devices can be repaired, reused, and kept in circulation longer.

sustainability.visitorExpLink: See how this shapes the visitor experience

cta.title: Build a more sustainable visitor experience.
cta.desc: See how Tree'd is designed to avoid unnecessary processing and keep devices in use longer through efficient AI and modular hardware.

hero.label: Careers
hero.desc: We’re building AI audio guide systems for museums and looking for craft-obsessed individuals to join a small, high-impact team. No bureaucracy, just pure building.

careers.valuesDesc: Tree'd builds AI audio guide systems for museums. Visitors tap exhibits, listen to curated stories, and ask questions to get answers instantly, in their language, without screens or apps.

respect.title: Respect Over Disruption
respect.desc: We design with deep respect for cultural spaces and the stories they hold. Tree'd exists to nurture the visitor–exhibit relationship, and never interrupt it.

simple.title: Simple Yet Powerful
simple.desc: We believe the best ideas don't need to announce themselves. We build intuitive products that are understood in seconds, not explained in steps.

curiosity.title: Curiosity Comes First
curiosity.desc: We believe questions are where the experience begins. Tree'd is built to reward curiosity, whether it comes from a visitor in a gallery or a designer on our team.

intent.title: Build With Intent
intent.desc: Tree'd works in spaces where silence matters and distraction isn't an option. That's why we build with care. Every detail must serve the overall experience.

process.title: Our Application Process
process.subtitle: We don't do rigid job titles. If you're craft-obsessed and you build, we want to hear from you, always. We accept applications all year round to join our high-impact team.
process.note: When applying, please mention the function or area you're most interested in (e.g., software, marketing, sales, design, operations.)

steps.title: Send your application
steps.desc: Tell us what excites you and what you've built. Add your CV and anything that proves your work.

steps.title: Quick conversation
steps.desc: A short call to understand how you think, communicate, and collaborate.

steps.title: Practical case
steps.desc: A real-world exercise designed to reflect the kind of work you would actually do. Clear scope, reasonable time.

steps.title: Join the team
steps.desc: Receive the offer. Most hires wrap in under 2 weeks.

hero.label: Who We Are
hero.desc: Tree'd builds AI audio guide systems for museums. Visitors tap exhibits, listen to curated stories, and ask questions to get answers instantly, in their language, without screens or apps.

careers.label: Careers
careers.desc: If our mission, vision, and values resonate with you and you want to work at the intersection of culture, technology, and human experience, Tree'd might be the place for you. We're building the next generation of audio guides for museums and cultural institutions worldwide, and we're always looking for curious, driven people to grow with us.

whatWeDo.heading: We build the entire system ourselves, from electronics to software to physical design.
whatWeDo.subheading: Every layer is developed in-house, so the entire experience works as a single, coherent ecosystem.

cards.title: Electronics & Embedded Systems
cards.body: We design our own boards, select each component deliberately, and engineer efficient circuit boards built for reliability, performance, and continuous use in demanding environments.

cards.title: Software & AI Infrastructure
cards.body: We develop the full software stack, from embedded firmware to on-device conversational AI, to the backend and dashboard, ensuring speed, stability, and seamless interaction.

cards.title: Industrial Design & Manufacturing
cards.body: We design and produce our own housings with a focus on sustainability, durability, ergonomics, and aesthetics, ensuring each device is intuitive to use and built to withstand heavy daily use.

mission.label: Mission
mission.title: To build, develop, and implement intuitive, multilingual solutions that enable museums to offer more immersive, engaging experiences.

commitment.title: Our Commitment
commitment.desc: Culture is not static, and museum experiences should not be either. Thus, we're committed to building systems that let visitors explore freely, engage more deeply, and ask as many questions as they desire, without barriers of language or format.

vision.title: Vision
vision.desc: To make culture and history exploration feel personal, everywhere.

values.label: Our Values
values.heading: The principles that guide our craft.

curiosity.title: Curiosity Comes First
curiosity.desc: Questions are where the experience begins. Tree'd is built to reward curiosity.

intent.title: Build with Intent
intent.desc: We work in spaces where silence matters. Every detail must serve the visitor experience.

respect.title: Respect Over Disruption
respect.desc: We nurture the visitor-exhibit relationship and never interrupt it.

simple.title: Simple Yet Powerful
simple.desc: The best ideas don't announce themselves. Intuitive products, understood in seconds.

paragraph.line1: These are what shape how we think, design, and build.

cards.heading: Museums
cards.body: Museums hold centuries of meaning. They shouldn’t be limited to static playback. They deserve tools that help visitors truly understand what they’re looking at.

cards.heading: Curiosity
cards.body: Visitors learn through stories, but their curiosity goes far beyond them. Current tour solutions stop at the script. We build for a limitless layer of questions.

cards.heading: Tech to Educate
cards.body: We’re inspired by building tech that exists to deepen understanding. Across art, history, and civilizations, it should help people learn, not distract them.

cards.heading: Access for All
cards.body: No one should feel lost in a museum. We’re driven by making culture accessible, regardless of language, background, or prior knowledge.

revenue.label: Financial Models
revenue.title: Turn Every Visit into
revenue.subtitle: Revenue
revenue.desc: Tree'd is designed to be a self-sustaining asset for your museum. While we offer standard licensing for institutions with available budget, we specialize in a frictionless revenue-sharing model.
revenue.desc2: If your budget is tight, we provide the hardware and infrastructure at a minimal upfront cost, sharing a portion of the visitor guide fees. This transforms our service into a net-positive revenue stream from day one.

models.title: Revenue Sharing
models.desc: Minimal upfront cost. We grow when you grow.

models.title: Direct Licensing
models.desc: Institutional licensing with fixed annual fees.

handset.title: The Handset
handset.desc: The visitor's companion. A screen-free, vintage-inspired audio device that lets visitors tap exhibits and ask follow-up questions in real time.

latency.title: Lightning Fast

tree.desc: The dependable backbone of your museum audio guide system. It securely distributes audio tracks, AI knowledge, and firmware updates to every Handset.

dashboard.title: The Dashboard

text_block.top: Traditional audio guides are a one-way street.

why_different.title: Why Tree'd is different
why_different.subtitle: A museum audio guide system designed for two-way exploration — without screens, apps, or scheduling.

static.title: What is a conversational audio guide?
static.para1: A conversational museum guide is an audio guide that allows visitors to ask questions and receive answers in real time, instead of listening to fixed recordings.
static.para2: Visitors explore at their own pace, ask follow-up questions, and receive responses in their language, based on curated museum content.

hero.label: Revenue Model
hero.title: Turn every visit
hero.subtitle: into revenue.
hero.desc: A revenue-sharing model for our AI audio guides with minimal upfront cost, aligned with your museum’s growth.

philosophy.label: The Foundation
philosophy.title: BUILT TO GROW TOGETHER
philosophy.desc: Revenue share is calculated using a dynamic equation. There is no flat percentage applied to all museums. We grow as you grow.

primary.label: Major Variable
primary.title: PRIMARY DRIVER
primary.desc: Annual Visitor Volume is our primary metric. Higher annual visitor numbers result in a progressively higher museum share.

waived.title: WAIVED UPFRONT COSTS
waived.desc: We minimize financial barriers to innovation, subject to the size and scope of your project.

calculator.title: Estimate Your Audio Guide Revenue
calculator.desc: Use the calculator below to estimate potential revenue from AI audio guides based on your annual visitors and expected adoption rate.

labels.adoptionTip: The percentage of visitors who use the audio guide during their visit.
labels.shareTip: Your portion of the total revenue generated, determined by our dynamic scaling equation.
labels.priceTip: This is the price we'll agree upon to charge to visitors to use the Tree'd guided tour.

statements.title: No voice recordings, ever.
statements.body: Conversations with the guide are processed in real time and discarded immediately. Nothing is stored on the Handset. Nothing leaves the museum.

statements.title: No visitor profiles, ever.
statements.body: The dashboard shows you patterns across your audience — never data tied to an individual. You learn what your visitors do, not who they are.

statements.title: On-premise, by design.
statements.body: All data is processed locally on The Tree, your on-site AI hub. It never touches an external server. You control what is collected, how it is used, and who can see it.

statements.title: GDPR-compliant, by architecture.
statements.body: Most tools comply with GDPR through consent banners and data agreements. Tree'd complies because the data never exists in a form that requires consent in the first place.

actions.desc: Here's what each metrics category actually tells you and how to utilize it,

cards.action: Know which spaces are working and which aren't — then adapt.
cards.body: See where visitors slow down, what themes they keep returning to, and which galleries lose them. Now you have a reason to rearrange.

cards.action: See what visitors actually engage with.
cards.body: See which audio stories get finished, which ones are skipped, and what visitors ask next. You'll quickly spot what holds attention and what needs improvement.
decisions: Add depth where curiosity is high

cards.action: Turn assumptions into clear audience insight.
cards.body: See which languages visitors use, how that changes over time, and which groups engage with which exhibits.

cards.action: Your staff manages visitors. Not hardware.
cards.body: Battery levels, docking status, signal stability: all visible before the doors open. No surprises on the floor.
decisions: Decide the number of devices required

hero.desc: Clear answers about Tree'd, the ecosystem, and how deployments work for museums.

items.q: What is an AI museum audio guide?
items.a: An AI museum audio guide is a conversational system that lets visitors explore exhibitions at their own pace, listen to curated stories, and ask follow-up questions in real time. Instead of playing fixed recordings, it responds dynamically based on visitor curiosity—making the [visitor experience](/visitor-experience) more personal, interactive, and engaging.

items.q: How does Tree'd work for visitors?
items.a: Visitors simply pick up a [Handset](/handset), select their language by tapping a panel, and start exploring. When they see an exhibit with a Tree'd sensor, they tap it to hear the story. They can then ask the Handset any follow-up questions naturally.
Tree'd currently supports 12+ major languages with native-level AI fluency, including English, Dutch, French, German, Spanish, Italian, and more. We are constantly adding new languages.

items.q: Do visitors need to download an app?
items.a: No. Tree'd is a physical, screen-free system. There are no apps to download, no accounts to create, and no personal devices required.

items.q: Is the system accessible for all ages?
items.a: Yes. The [visitor experience](/visitor-experience) is intentionally simple—just tap and talk. It's intuitive for children, seniors, and everyone in between.

items.q: How long does a Handset battery last?
items.a: Up to 6 hours of active use, with fast-charging that brings the device back to 50% in about an hour, allowing [the Handset](/handset) to comfortably cover a full museum day.

items.q: How many Handsets can a single Tree support?
items.a: This depends on your configuration. [Trees](/tree) are modular and sized to your required Handset count.

items.q: Does Tree'd require a special team to operate?
items.a: No. Tree'd was designed so museums can run the system without additional technical staff.

items.q: Can Tree'd work in high-traffic museums?
items.a: Yes. Charging capacity, syncing behavior, and local processing are engineered for continuous, [large-volume use](/use-cases).

items.q: Does Tree'd require a stable internet connection?
items.a: Only for initial setup and periodic cloud updates. Day-to-day usage runs entirely on your local network, so outages don't affect visitor experience.

items.q: Where does the AI model run?
items.a: Locally. The [Tree](/tree) hosts your museum's knowledge set, language models, and updates — nothing depends on external servers during use. This allows the system to use less energy and deliver faster answers using [conversational AI](/solution).

items.q: Can we control what the AI says?
items.a: Yes. All AI knowledge is museum-approved and curated. The model stays within your defined content boundaries and NEVER looks up information from the internet or external sources.

items.q: Can Tree'd answer questions specific to our collection?
items.a: Yes. We build a [custom knowledge base](/solution) for each museum, covering your artworks, themes, and context.

items.q: How accurate are the AI answers?
items.a: 100% accurate. Answers follow museum-approved content, ensuring they stay factual, appropriate, and aligned with your curatorial standards. This leaves 0% chance of AI hallucinations.
[The Tree](/tree) connects to power and your local network. Handsets are then paired to the network and will not require further setup. Most museums are fully operational within hours.
Yes. We offer [pilot programs](/booking) depending on the region and availability.

items.q: Can the aesthetic of the Hub (The Tree) be customized for our building or exhibition?
items.a: Yes. [The Tree's](/tree) physical design, materials, and finish can be tailored to your museum or created in collaboration with a local artist.

items.q: Do you offer revenue-sharing or subscription models?
items.a: Yes. Tree'd offers flexible commercial models, including [revenue sharing](/revenue-sharing) and straightforward subscriptions. Revenue-sharing percentages vary based on your museum's annual visitor volume. For museums that prefer predictable budgeting, we also offer a clear subscription model that covers hardware, software, multimedia, and the entire Tree'd ecosystem.

items.q: Is Tree'd compliant with privacy regulations?
items.a: Yes. Privacy-by-design is at the heart of our architecture. We never collect personal data from visitors, don't store voice recordings, and process all [analytics](/dashboard) anonymously on-site. This makes Tree'd a safe and compliant choice for museums evaluating AI visitor technologies.

questions.q: What is an AI museum audio guide?
questions.a: An AI museum audio guide is a conversational system that lets visitors explore exhibitions at their own pace, listen to curated stories, and ask follow-up questions in real time. Instead of playing fixed recordings, it responds dynamically based on visitor curiosity—making the experience more personal, interactive, and engaging.

questions.q: How does an AI museum audio guide work?
questions.a: Visitors start by selecting their language, then tap a Handset on an exhibit to hear its story. After listening, they can ask questions naturally, and the AI responds instantly using museum-approved content. The entire experience is seamless, self-paced, and requires no setup.

questions.q: What is the difference between AI and traditional audio guides?
questions.a: Traditional audio guides play pre-recorded, one-way content. AI audio guides create two-way conversations. Visitors can ask questions, explore topics in more depth, and control how much they learn—turning a passive tour into an interactive experience.

questions.q: Do museum audio guides support multiple languages?
questions.a: Yes. AI museum audio guides support multiple languages simultaneously, allowing each visitor to experience the museum in their preferred language. This expands accessibility and ensures a consistent experience for international audiences.

questions.q: Do visitors need to download an app to use it?
questions.a: No. The system is completely screen-free and requires no downloads or setup. Visitors simply pick up the device, choose their language, and start exploring immediately.

1.title: What Is an AI Museum Audio Guide?
1.excerpt: Most visitors think an audio guide plays a script. An AI guide answers the question they didn't plan to ask. What that means for museums, explained clearly.

2.title: What Is a Conversational Audio Guide?
2.excerpt: A conversational guide doesn't play a script. It listens, responds, and adapts. Here's what that actually means for your museum floor.

3.title: How Much Does a Museum Audio Guide Cost?
3.excerpt: There is no fixed number — and understanding why is more useful than any price list. A comprehensive look at the cost drivers that actually determine what a museum audio guide system will cost your institution.

4.title: AI Audio Guide vs Traditional Audio Guide: What's Actually Different
4.excerpt: The handset looks similar. The experience is nothing alike. A direct, honest comparison of what AI guides do differently — and where traditional guides still hold their ground.

5.title: Museum Audio Guide vs Mobile App: Which Is Right for Your Institution?
5.excerpt: Apps promise flexibility. Dedicated guides promise reliability. This comparison helps directors decide which model fits their visitor profile, budget, and operational constraints.

6.title: Multilingual Visitors, One Museum: How Audio Guides Handle Language Access
6.excerpt: Most museums offer their guide in three or four languages and call it multilingual. Their international visitors experience something different. A look at how language access actually works across every major guide format — and what it takes to serve visitors in the language they actually think in.

7.title: NFC Tags vs Number Entry in Museum Audio Guides: What the Activation Choice Actually Means for Visitors
7.excerpt: Number entry and NFC tapping are two ways to trigger the same content — but they produce different visitor experiences. An honest comparison of both activation methods, what each requires to work well, and why the choice matters more than most guide procurement processes acknowledge.

8.title: Why Better Audio Guide Content Won’t Fix Visitor Engagement
8.excerpt: When visitor engagement is low, the instinct is to improve the content — better scripts, richer production, more languages. It rarely works. The constraint isn’t the content. It’s the architecture. A perspective from inside the problem.

9.title: Why Museum Visitors Stop Using Audio Guides — And What Actually Keeps Them Engaged
9.excerpt: Most visitors pick up the guide, use it for a few stops, and put it down. The problem isn’t the content. It’s the format. A look at why disengagement happens — and what changes when visitors can finally ask questions.

10.title: The Screen Problem in Museum Galleries: Why the Best Guide Keeps Eyes on the Art
10.excerpt: Asking visitors to use their smartphones as museum guides means competing for attention with every notification, app, and distraction on the same device. A look at what screen-based engagement costs in a gallery context — and why it matters which direction the handset points.

11.title: The Questions Visitors Never Get to Ask (And What Happens When They Can)
11.excerpt: For decades, museums have designed interpretation around what curators assume visitors want to know. Conversational guides reveal what visitors actually ask. The gap between those two things is one of the most important things the museum sector hasn’t been looking at.

12.title: The 90-Day Museum Audio Guide Pilot: What to Expect and How to Measure It
12.excerpt: A 90-day pilot removes the guesswork. This guide walks through what to track, what to expect, and how to present results to your leadership team.

13.title: How Museum Directors Should Evaluate Audio Guides in 2026
13.excerpt: Most audio guide evaluations ask the wrong questions. A framework focused on the 7 decisions that separate guides visitors actually use from ones that quietly underperform.

14.title: Beyond Language: What Museum Audio Guide Accessibility Really Requires
14.excerpt: Multilingual support is one dimension of audio guide accessibility. It is not the whole picture. A look at what genuinely accessible guide design requires — physical, cognitive, and sensory — and why most systems address only the most visible part of the problem.

15.title: Subscription, Licence, or Per-Visit: How Museum Audio Guide Pricing Models Actually Work
15.excerpt: The price a vendor quotes and the price a museum pays over five years are rarely the same number. A clear-eyed guide to how audio guide pricing models are structured, what each one means in practice, and which questions to ask before the contract is signed.

16.title: Tree'd Signs Museum Partnership with Fayoum Art Center
16.excerpt: Tree'd announces its first commercial museum partnership in Egypt — signing with Fayoum Art Center, home to MENA’s first caricature museum, just one month after entering the market commercially.

hero.subtext: A system designed for every role, every context, and every moment inside the museum.

cases.title: Built for every kind of museum.

items.title: High-Traffic Museums
items.problem: Large crowds. Dozens of languages. Not enough staff to give every visitor the experience they came for.
items.solution: Visitors navigate at their own pace and access content in their language instantly — without relying on staff.
items.outcome: Smoother flow. Consistent quality. Every visitor, every time.

items.title: Art Museums
items.problem: Wall labels say what something is. They rarely explain why it matters — or address what visitors actually want to know.
items.solution: Visitors converse freely with the guide about specific works and go as deep as their curiosity takes them.
items.outcome: More time per artwork. A deeper, more personal connection to the collection.

items.title: Science & Educational Museums
items.problem: A child and a researcher need fundamentally different explanations. Static content can't adapt to either.
items.solution: The guide responds to each visitor's questions at their level — not the demographic assumed.
items.outcome: Stronger learning outcomes across every age group and knowledge level.

items.title: Smaller & Private Museums
items.problem: Limited staff and budget make multilingual or guided experiences feel out of reach.
items.solution: A plug-and-play system that delivers professional, multilingual experiences without additional staffing.
items.outcome: A world-class visitor experience. Without the operational complexity.

items.title: Temporary Exhibitions
items.problem: Exhibition content changes constantly. Traditional audio guides can't keep up without significant cost and effort.
items.solution: Content updates remotely and instantly — no new hardware, no reinstallation, no downtime.
items.outcome: Full flexibility on content and utilization. Always up-to-date.

privacyPolicy.intro: ZedZag B.V. ("ZedZag", "we", "us", or "our") is committed to protecting your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect through the Tree'd History Guide website (treed.co), why we collect it, and your rights under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). For information about the cookies we use, please also see our Cookie Policy.
privacyPolicy.s1Body: ZedZag B.V. is incorporated under Dutch law (KvK registration number 76255220), VAT (BTW) identification number: NL860563443B02, with its registered office at Plantage Kerklaan 21, 1018 SZ Amsterdam, Netherlands. For the purposes of the GDPR, ZedZag B.V. is the data controller for personal data collected through this Website. If you have any questions about how we handle your data, please contact us at hello@treed.co.
privacyPolicy.s2Intro: We collect the following categories of personal data:
s2Sub2Items: Details about your museum project: visitor numbers, number of handsets, number of artefacts, languages required, preferred launch timeline, and preferred commercial model
Any additional information you choose to provide
s2Sub3Items: Pages visited and time spent on each page
Google Analytics identifiers (see Section 5 and our Cookie Policy)
privacyPolicy.s3Title: 3. Why We Collect It and Our Legal Basis
privacyPolicy.s3Sub1Body: We process the data you submit through the Contact form in order to respond to your message. Our legal basis is legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f) GDPR): ZedZag has a legitimate interest in engaging with museum professionals who reach out to us, and this interest is not overridden by your rights.
privacyPolicy.s3Sub2Body: We process the data you submit through the Request Pricing form to prepare a tailored pricing proposal and to follow up with you about your museum's requirements. Our legal basis is the performance of a contract or the taking of steps at your request prior to entering into a contract (Article 6(1)(b) GDPR), as a pricing request constitutes a pre-contractual step. Where Article 6(1)(b) does not apply, we rely on legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f) GDPR).
privacyPolicy.s3Sub3Body: We use Google Analytics 4 to understand how visitors use our Website and to improve its content and performance. Our legal basis for analytics cookies and associated data collection is consent (Article 6(1)(a) GDPR). We rely on consent for analytics cookies and associated data collection; all other processing described in this section is based on legitimate interest unless stated otherwise. We will ask for your consent before placing analytics cookies on your device via our cookie banner. You may withdraw your consent at any time through the "Cookie Settings" option on our Website. We have configured Google Analytics 4 in a privacy-friendly manner: we have accepted Google's Data Processing Amendment, IP addresses are anonymised by default within GA4 before any data is stored, and we have disabled data sharing with Google for their own advertising or product improvement purposes. For more details on which cookies are used and how to manage them, see our Cookie Policy.
privacyPolicy.s4Title: 4. How Long We Keep Your Data
s4Items: Contact form and pricing request data: up to 24 months from the date of your enquiry, or until commercial discussions are concluded — and no longer than 24 months from last contact, unless retention is required otherwise by law.
Google Analytics data: in accordance with Google’s standard retention settings (up to 14 months for user-level and event-level data).
privacyPolicy.s5Title: 5. Who We Share Your Data With
privacyPolicy.s5Intro: We do not sell your personal data. We may share it with:
s5Items: Members of our team responsible for sales and customer enquiries, using cloud collaboration tools (e.g., Google Workspace) that act as data processors.

Google LLC, for Google Analytics 4 (analytics) and Google Maps (embedded map in our footer). Google may process data on servers in the United States. Google LLC participates in the EU–U.S. Data Privacy Framework, which provides an adequate level of protection. For more information, see Google’s Privacy Policy at policies.google.com/privacy.
Professional advisors (lawyers, accountants) where necessary for legal or financial compliance.
Regulatory authorities where required by applicable law.

We have entered into appropriate data processing agreements with our service providers where required by GDPR. Google LLC acts as a data processor for analytics data; we have executed Google's standard data processing terms to ensure adequate protection.
privacyPolicy.s6Body: Google Analytics 4 may transfer data to Google servers in the United States. Google LLC participates in the EU–U.S. Data Privacy Framework (and equivalent UK framework), which the European Commission has recognised as providing an adequate level of data protection. We do not transfer your form submission data outside the European Economic Area.
privacyPolicy.s7Intro: As a data subject in the EU or UK, you have the following rights:
s7Items: Right of access: you can request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Right to rectification: you can ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
Right to erasure: you can ask us to delete your data where there is no longer a lawful basis for us to retain it.
Right to restriction: you can ask us to restrict processing of your data in certain circumstances.
Right to data portability: you can request your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Right to object: you can object to processing based on legitimate interest.
Right to withdraw consent: where we rely on consent as the legal basis for processing, you can withdraw it at any time without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.
privacyPolicy.s7Exercise: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at hello@treed.co. We may ask you to verify your identity before fulfilling your request, in order to protect your data from unauthorised access. You can withdraw your consent for analytics and third-party cookies at any time through the "Cookie Settings" option available on our Website — withdrawing consent is as easy as giving it. We will respond to data subject requests within 30 days. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (AP), the Dutch Data Protection Authority, at www.autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl.
privacyPolicy.s8Body: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, accidental loss, alteration, disclosure or destruction. Where data is transmitted over the internet, we use encrypted connections (HTTPS).
privacyPolicy.s9Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will post the revised version on this page with an updated “last updated” date. We encourage you to review this page periodically.
privacyPolicy.s10Body: For any questions about this Privacy Policy or your personal data, please contact us at hello@treed.co, or write to us at: ZedZag B.V., Plantage Kerklaan 21, 1018 SZ Amsterdam, Netherlands.
privacyPolicy.s3Sub4Body: Our Website embeds Google Maps to display our office location. This service is only loaded after you have provided your consent to third-party cookies via our cookie banner. Our legal basis is consent (Article 6(1)(a) GDPR).
privacyPolicy.introPre: ZedZag B.V. ("ZedZag", "we", "us", or "our") is committed to protecting your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect through the Tree'd History Guide website (treed.co), why we collect it, and your rights under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). For information about the cookies we use, please also see our Cookie Policy

cookiePolicy.intro: This Cookie Policy explains what cookies are, which cookies the Tree’d History Guide website (treed.co) uses, and how you can manage them. This Website is operated by ZedZag B.V., Plantage Kerklaan 21, 1018 SZ Amsterdam, Netherlands.
cookiePolicy.s1Body: Cookies are small text files placed on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile phone) when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work, or work more efficiently, and to provide information to the site operator. Cookies are not programs — they cannot carry viruses or access information on your device beyond what was placed there by the website.
cookiePolicy.s2Intro: We use the following categories of cookies:
cookiePolicy.s2Cat1Body: These cookies are essential for the Website to function and cannot be disabled. They enable core functionality such as security and session management. They do not store any personally identifiable information.
cookiePolicy.s2Cat2Body: We use Google Analytics 4 (GA4) to understand how visitors interact with our Website. GA4 places the following cookies on your device:
s2Cat2Items: _ga — Used to distinguish unique users by assigning a randomly generated number as a client identifier. Duration: 2 years.
_ga_NFC69M5MYC — Used to persist session state. Duration: 1 year.
cookiePolicy.s2Cat2Note: Google acts as a data processor on our behalf for analytics data, processing it according to our instructions. Google Analytics may transfer data to Google's servers in the United States. Google LLC participates in the EU–U.S. Data Privacy Framework. For more information, see the Google Analytics privacy overview at support.google.com/analytics/answer/6004245.
cookiePolicy.s2Cat3Body: Our website embeds a Google Maps map in the footer section to display our office location. Google Maps is only loaded after you have provided consent to third-party cookies through our cookie banner. When the map loads, Google may set cookies on your device subject to Google's own Cookie Policy at policies.google.com/technologies/cookies. We have no control over those cookies.
cookiePolicy.s3Intro: You can control and manage cookies in several ways:
cookiePolicy.s3Sub1Body: Most browsers allow you to view, manage, delete, and block cookies. Here is how to access cookie settings in common browsers:
s3BrowserItems: Google Chrome: Settings → Privacy and security → Cookies and other site data
Mozilla Firefox: Settings → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data
Microsoft Edge: Settings → Cookies and site permissions
Opera: Settings → Advanced → Privacy & security → Site settings → Cookies
cookiePolicy.s3Warning: Please note that blocking or deleting cookies may affect your experience on some websites. Disabling analytics or third-party cookies will not prevent you from using the Tree’d website.
cookiePolicy.s4Body: We may update this Cookie Policy when we change the cookies we use or when applicable law requires us to do so. Please check this page periodically. The date at the top of this page shows when it was last revised.
cookiePolicy.s5Body: If you have any questions about our use of cookies, please contact us at hello@treed.co or write to ZedZag B.V., Plantage Kerklaan 21, 1018 SZ Amsterdam, Netherlands.
cookiePolicy.s2bBody: We will request your consent before placing non-essential cookies — including analytics and third-party cookies — on your device, where required by applicable EU law (ePrivacy Directive and GDPR). You can accept or reject these cookies through our cookie consent banner, which appears on your first visit. Non-essential cookies are not placed on your device until you have provided your consent. You may withdraw or change your consent at any time through the "Cookie Settings" option available on our Website, or by adjusting your browser settings as described in Section 4 below. Withdrawing consent is as easy as giving it. Strictly necessary cookies do not require your consent and cannot be disabled, as they are essential for the Website to function.

cookieTable.name: _ga
cookieTable.purpose: Statistics — distinguishes unique users by assigning a randomly generated client identifier

cookieTable.name: _ga_NFC69M5MYC
cookieTable.purpose: Statistics — persists session state for Google Analytics

cookieTable.name: NID / SID
cookieTable.purpose: Map functionality and user preferences for Google Maps

cookiePolicy.s2bRejectNote: Users have the right to refuse all non-essential cookies. Choosing "Reject All" will not limit your access to the core features of this Website. The option to reject cookies is presented with equal visual prominence to the option to accept them.

termsOfUse.intro: Please read these Terms of Use carefully before using the Tree'd History Guide website at treed.co (Website). By accessing or using the Website, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree to these Terms, please do not use the Website. Use of this Website is also governed by our Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy, which explain how we collect, use, and store your data and how cookies are used.
termsOfUse.s1Body: This Website is operated by ZedZag B.V. termsOfUse.s5Body: TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ZEDZAG B.V. SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, USE, GOODWILL, OR OTHER INTANGIBLE LOSSES, ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH: (A) YOUR ACCESS TO OR USE OF (OR INABILITY TO ACCESS OR USE) THE WEBSITE; (B) ANY CONTENT OBTAINED FROM THE WEBSITE; OR (C) UNAUTHORISED ACCESS TO OR ALTERATION OF YOUR DATA. termsOfUse.s7Body: We do not guarantee that the Website will be available at all times or that access will be uninterrupted. We may suspend, withdraw, or restrict the availability of all or any part of the Website for business, operational, or technical reasons without notice.

termsOfUse.s9Body: You agree, to the extent permitted by applicable law, to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless ZedZag B.V. and its directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or in connection with: (a) your use of or access to the Website; (b) your breach of these Terms of Use; or (c) any content you submit through the Website. termsOfUse.s9bBody: We shall not be liable for any failure or delay in the performance of our obligations under these Terms caused by events beyond our reasonable control, including but not limited to: technical failures, internet outages, power failures, failures of third-party suppliers or infrastructure providers, strikes or industrial action, acts of government or regulatory authority, natural disasters, pandemic, civil unrest, or any other cause beyond our reasonable control. termsOfUse.s11Body: We may revise these Terms of Use at any time by updating this page. The revised terms will take effect from the date they are posted. By continuing to use the Website after changes are posted, you accept the revised terms. We encourage you to check this page periodically.
termsOfUse.s12Body: These Terms of Use and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with them shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Netherlands. Any disputes shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts in Amsterdam, Netherlands.
termsOfUse.s12bBody: In the event of a dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms of Use, the parties agree to first attempt to resolve the matter through good-faith negotiations. Either party may initiate the process by giving written notice to the other party describing the dispute. The parties will have a period of 30 days from receipt of such notice to negotiate an amicable settlement before either party may commence legal proceedings. This obligation does not apply where a party requires urgent interim relief from a court.
termsOfUse.s14Body: If you have any questions about these Terms of Use, please contact us at hello@treed.co or write to ZedZag B.V., Plantage Kerklaan 21, 1018 SZ Amsterdam, Netherlands.

cookieBanner.title: We use cookies
cookieBanner.body: We use 1 partner (Google) to understand how visitors use our site so we can keep improving it. We also use cookies to show our office location on a map. Non-essential cookies are only placed with your consent.
